agent_output: {"summary":{"verdict":"BLOCK","one_liner":"Race condition between stock check and decrement will oversell inventory under concurrent requests"},"critical_findings":[{"title":"TOCTOU Race Condition: Stock Check vs. Decrement","risk":"Multiple concurrent requests read the same stock value, all pass the check, then all decrement — selling more units than exist. With popular products on a public API, this WILL happen.","severity":"Critical","location":"addToCart lines 5-9 (getStock → check → addItem → decrementStock)","reproduction":"Product has 1 unit in stock. Two users call addToCart simultaneously. Both read stock=1, both pass the check, both add to cart, both decrement. Stock goes to -1. Two orders fulfilled for one unit.","fix":"Use an atomic compare-and-decrement operation (e.g., UPDATE products SET stock = stock - $qty WHERE id = $id AND stock >= $qty, checking affected rows == 1) or wrap the entire operation in a serializable transaction. The stock read and write must be a single atomic step.","time_to_fix":"30 minutes","acceptance_criteria_affected":"Inventory accuracy, order fulfillment correctness"},{"title":"Non-Atomic Cart Add + Stock Decrement: Partial Failure Leaves Inconsistent State","risk":"If addItem succeeds but decrementStock fails (DB error, timeout, connection drop), the item is in the user's cart but stock was never reduced. Other users can also buy the same units. Conversely, if decrementStock succeeds but the process crashes before completing, stock is lost with no cart entry.","severity":"Critical","location":"addToCart lines 8-9 (addItem then decrementStock as separate operations)","reproduction":"Call addToCart. addItem succeeds. Kill the DB connection before decrementStock executes. Cart has the item, stock is unchanged. Repeat N times — stock never decreases.","fix":"Wrap addItem and decrementStock in a single database transaction so both succeed or both roll back. Ideally combine with the atomic stock check from Finding 1 into one transaction.","time_to_fix":"30 minutes","acceptance_criteria_affected":"Data consistency between cart and inventory"},{"title":"No Input Validation on Public API Inputs","risk":"quantity can be 0, negative, fractional, or extremely large. A negative quantity would pass the stock check (stock is always >= negative number) and could increment stock or corrupt cart state depending on DB behavior. Zero quantity wastes a DB round-trip. No validation on userId/productId being non-empty.","severity":"High","location":"addToCart function signature and body — no validation before DB calls","reproduction":"Call addToCart(userId, productId, -5). stock (e.g., 10) >= -5 is true. decrementStock subtracts -5, effectively adding 5 to stock. Infinite inventory exploit.","fix":"Add validation at function entry: assert quantity is a positive integer, userId and productId are non-empty strings. Reject before touching the database.","time_to_fix":"10 minutes","acceptance_criteria_affected":"API security, inventory integrity"}],"important_issues":[{"title":"No idempotency protection","description":"If a client retries a failed/timed-out request, the same item gets added twice. Public APIs need an idempotency key to prevent duplicate cart additions.","location":"addToCart — no idempotency mechanism"},{"title":"Stock decrement is not tied to a reservation or hold","description":"Adding to cart permanently decrements stock. If the user never checks out or abandons the cart, that stock is locked forever. Consider a reservation with TTL instead of a hard decrement.","location":"decrementStock call with no expiry or reversal path"}],"suggestions":["Use SELECT ... FOR UPDATE or an atomic conditional update to eliminate the race window","Consider a reservation pattern: hold stock with a TTL, only permanently decrement on checkout","Add rate limiting per userId to mitigate abuse of the public endpoint"],"production_readiness":{"deployment_risks":"This code will oversell inventory from day one under any meaningful concurrent load. The race window is wide (two awaited DB calls between check and decrement).","rollback_capability":"No migration involved — rollback is straightforward at deploy level, but any oversold inventory during the window requires manual correction.","backward_compatibility":"N/A — new function","monitoring_coverage":"No logging or metrics. Add monitoring for stock-goes-negative events and failed decrements to catch inconsistencies.","breaking_changes":{"api_breaking":false,"db_destructive":false,"shared_library":false,"auth_security":false,"data_pipeline":false,"config_changes":false,"details":[]},"safe_to_deploy":false,"rollback_plan":"Revert deploy; audit and fix any negative stock or orphaned cart items created during exposure window."},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":97,"high_confidence_areas":["Race condition is textbook TOCTOU — certain to occur under concurrent load","Partial failure between addItem and decrementStock is a standard atomicity gap","Negative quantity exploit is trivially reproducible"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact DB driver behavior for negative decrements depends on schema constraints not shown"],"assumptions":["db operations are not already wrapped in an external transaction by the caller","decrementStock does a simple subtraction without a floor check"]}}
